english: magic formula. — grúb-pa lus (grub pa lus) Med. either: the frame, the structure of the body, or more prob. an abbreviation of jńiń-po lṅa-las grúb-pai lus (snying po lnga las grub pai lus) Med., v. jńiń-po (snying po). — 2. the state of perfection, grub-pa tob-pa (grub pa thob pa) to attain to this state, grub-ṭób (grub thob) सिद्ध one that has attained to it, a saint; grub-brnyés (grub brnyes), grub-mčóg (grub mchog) id.; grub mtá (grub mtha') (C.col. *ḍhum-tá* (*dhum ta*)) Ssk. सिद्धान्त opinion, theory Zam.; ṗyi-naṅ-gi grub-mta ma čáms-par (phyi nang gi grub mtha' ma mchams par) Glr. there being no conformity of opinion between Brahmanists and Buddhists; also n. of a philosophical work, Was. 262. — ma-grúb-pa (ma grub pa), grub-pa-méd-pa (grub pa med pa)?

1. S.g. n. of a burrowing animal, Sch.: badger. — 2. pf. of grúm-pa (grum pa) lamed, crippled, grúm-po (grum po) a maimed person, a cripple; grúm-bu (grum bu), grum-nád (grum nad) gout, rheumatism, = tsig-nád (tshig nad); drag-grúm (drag grum) gout, podagra; *ṡa-ḍúm* (*sha dum*) W., a feeling of lameness in the limbs.
a thick woolen blanket Mil.nt.
a class of demons, grul-búm-mo (grul bum mo) female demons; there are also horned demons of this kind.
C. a yak two or three years old.
a Naksatra, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar).
C. a sheet of paper (W. *ṡòg-gáṅ* (*shog gang*))

the fore-part of the neck, the throat, both the wind-pipe and the gullet; *ḍé-wa dé-mo* (*de wa de mo*), or *nyán-pa dug* (*nyan pa dug*) W. he has a good voice, sings well; gre (-ba) gágs(-pa) (gre (-ba) gags(-pa)) Med. hoarseness; *ḍé-wa táṅ-ce* (*de wa tang ce*) Ld. to join in singing or shouting; gré-ba dár-ba (gre ba dar ba) a snoring or rattling in the throat; *ḍe-bsál táṅ-ce* (*de bsal tang ce*) W. to hawk, to hem, to clear the throat.
a species of demons; gré-mo (gre mo) 1. female demons of this kind. — 2. v. grú-mo (gru mo).
vulg. for grá-ma (gra ma) awn.
W. ant, emmet.
pea, pease, món-sran-greu (mon sran gre'u) acc. to Wdn. = माष.
the flashing, lightening, shining Schr.

1. wheat, gro-yós (gro yos) parched grains of wheat, parched corn; gro-sóg (gro sog) stalk of wheat, wheat-straw. — 2. breakfast, taken late in the forenoon or about noon, gro ḍégs-pa (gro degs pa) Glr., also *ḍho bóg-pa* (*dho bog pa*) C. to take breakfast, = tsál-ma zá-ba (tshal ma za ba). — *ḍo źig* (*do zhig*) W. a morning's march, short day's march, reaching quarters already at 10 or 11 o'cl. a.m.
W. also *ḍó-wa* (*do wa*), the thin bark of the birch-tree, frq. used to write on (esp letters), or for ornamenting bows etc. Mil.
reddish gray.
1. = gro 2. — 2. n. of a medicinal herb Wdn. — 3. *ḍhó-ma* (*dho ma*), gya-ḍhó* (gya dho*) C. potato.
(Lex. श्वभ्र) 1. a deep dell, ravine, lateral valley C.; gróg-ču (grog chu) brook, rivulet; grog-yzár (grog gzar) a torrent pouring down in a ravine. — 2. W. = gróg-ča (grog cha).
ant, emmet; grog-tsáṅ (grog tshang), grog-mkár (grog mkhar) ant-hill; grog-spúr (grog spur) acc. to some = gróg-ma (grog ma), acc. to others some other insect.
n. of a medicine Wdn.

col. *rog* (*rog*) 1. friend; the more definite form is grógs-po (grogs po), fem. grógs-mo (grogs mo); ka-grógs (ka grogs) a seeming friend, a false friend; gtiṅ-grogs (gting grogs) a true friend; sdig-pai grógs-po-la rten-na (sdig pai grogs po la rten na) if he attaches himself to bad friends Dzl.; snyiṅ-gi grógs-po (snying gi grogs po) intimate friend, bosom-friend Pth.; grógs-po(r) byéd-pa (grogs po(r) byed pa) to make friendship, to enter into connexion with, to make a league, mṭoṅ-ma-ṗrád-pai grógs-po byas (mthong ma phrad pai grogs po byas), they joined in friendship without knowing each other Glr. — kye grógs-po ho (kye grogs po ho), friend! Pth. — 2. associate, companion, comrade, grógs-po-dag (grogs po dag) company, society Dzl. also used as address: comrades! friends! or more respectfully: honoured friends! honoured
